page: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based instance powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor family, utilizing 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The instance features a Nitro hypervisor, 64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It includes a bundled NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, but does not feature any local NVMe storage.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-core and graphics-related workloads. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48,574.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,551.0. Ray tracing and image processing benchmarks also fall into the average tier, with scores of 26,341.0 and 7,812.0 respectively. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the weak category. Memory performance is mixed, showing high cached read bandwidth but a weak PassMark memory score of 1,813.51.

For machine learning workloads, the T4G GPU supports moderate language model inference. It processes prompts for a 7B parameter model at 31.98 tokens per second and generates text at 15.24 tokens per second, both representing average tier performance. However, text generation for smaller 135M models is relatively poor at 42.38 tokens per second. Given the dedicated ARM cores and GPU acceleration, this instance is suited for graphics-intensive applications, Android game streaming, and entry-to-mid-level machine learning inference, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or memory-heavy database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based instance designed for graphics-intensive and GPU-accelerated workloads. Powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, it features 64.0 GB of system memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The hardware configuration includes a bundled NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, though it lacks local instance storage. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and graphics processing capabilities, balanced by weaker single-core and memory bandwidth scores. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for ARM-native graphics rendering, Android game streaming, and moderate machine learning inference workloads, such as prompt processing and text generation for medium-sized models, where dedicated physical cores and GPU acceleration are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM, optimized for graphics and machine learning.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge features 32 dedicated ARM64 cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for graphics-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and hardware-accelerated graphics for efficient machine learning and streaming workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
Bundled NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM
64.0 GB of system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local NVMe instance storage included
Suited for graphics-intensive applications, Android streaming, and mid-sized LLM inference
